## Required Context
Read `trinity/JULES_BRIEF.md` before starting. Treat it as the operating brief for Trinity's mission, proof discipline, quality bar, and merge-safety rules.

## Mission
<!-- What should this task accomplish for Trinity? Be specific. -->

## Why This Matters
<!-- Explain how this advances image, product, proof, distribution, or monetization. -->

## Strategic Lane
<!-- Pick one or two. -->
- [ ] Growth Kit / Distribution OS
- [ ] Growth Audit entry offer
- [ ] Trinity storefront
- [ ] Lab exhibit / proof artifact
- [ ] Catalog kit documentation
- [ ] Daniel brand bridge
- [ ] Repo hygiene

## Relevant Files
<!-- List the exact files Jules should inspect or edit. -->
- `trinity/JULES_BRIEF.md`

## Scope
<!-- What should be changed? -->

## Out Of Scope
Do not touch package files, lockfiles, dependencies, unrelated routes, or broad raw Trinity folders unless this issue explicitly asks for it.

## Proof-Safety Rules
- Do not invent customers, lead counts, revenue, conversion rates, savings, or production deployments.
- Label samples as fictional or simulated.
- Use "diagnostic", "designed to", "roadmap", "hypothesis", or "target outcome" when proof is not verified.
- Keep human review and implementation judgment visible.

## Acceptance Criteria
<!-- What must be true before the PR is merge-ready? -->
- [ ] PR is narrow and reviewable.
- [ ] PR follows `trinity/JULES_BRIEF.md`.
- [ ] No unverified claims are presented as facts.
- [ ] No unrelated package/dependency/lockfile changes.
- [ ] Relevant docs or links are updated.
- [ ] Verification is listed in the PR body.

## PR Body Requirements
The PR must explain:
- what changed;
- why it matters;
- proof-safety notes;
- verification performed;
- what was intentionally left untouched.
